title: CAMP KESEM UNIVERSITY OF OREGON - 211info
text: CAMP KESEM CAMP KESEM UNIVERSITY OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a week-long summer camp for children affected by a parent’s cancer that offers peer-based emotional support. Camp activities include games, arts and crafts, sports, water activities, and outdoor recreation. The camp is staffed by screened and trained college students. Eligibility: Ages 6-18 who have lost a parent to cancer, have a parent undergoing cancer treatment, or whose parent is a cancer survivor. Eligible children may return to Camp Kesem more than once. Children with disabilities and special needs are considered on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 525-0719 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (253) 736-3821 National Headquarters (541) 525-0719 Oregon Office Main phone (541) 525-0719 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website to register or more information. Applications open in January and are processed on a first-come, first-served basis. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Camps * Families/Friends of People with Cancer If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
